title: SourceManifest
description: One rule-catalog source manifest. Declares WHAT to fetch, from WHERE, at WHICH revision, and under WHICH license. Consumed by the collector pipeline (see docs/roadmap/rule-catalog-collection.md § Collector Architecture). One file per source, lives under rule-catalog/sources/<id>/manifest.yaml.

id.description: Stable source id used in every rule's `source` field. Matches the enum documented in the rule schema.

url_prefix.description: Canonical documentation URL prefix for the source.

license.description: SPDX id (e.g. `Apache-2.0`, `MIT`) or `LicenseRef-reference-only` for controls whose text is reserved (CIS, ISO, PCI, ...).

redistribution.description: `embeddable` = original text MAY ship in the normalized rule (permissive OSS license). `reference-only` = the collector authors an independent check and only cites the source via provenance.

kind.description: Fetch adapter to use. `git` for a git clone at a pinned revision, `http` for a single-URL download, `local` for a fixture-backed source (tests + a hand-authored source shipped in-repo).

revision.description: Immutable git commit SHA or artifact digest. MUST NOT be a mutable branch/tag.

subpath.description: Subdirectory within the fetched tree to include in the snapshot.

expected_sha256.description: Optional SHA-256 of the fetched artifact. The verifier compares and refuses on mismatch (kind=http primarily).

path.description: Filesystem path for kind=local (relative to repo root or absolute). Used by tests + hand-authored sources.

parser.description: Parser plugin id. Later phases register concrete parsers (`rego`, `azure-policy-json`, `checkov-yaml`, ...); the collector rejects an unknown value at load time.

cadence.description: How often the Phase 2 watcher re-fetches. `on-demand` = manual runs only.
